similar_questions.title: Find the Grid of Region Average

discussion_posts.name: strange_fellow
discussion_posts.content:
It is Easy only for those who knows the idea of prefix sum on a grid, 
Medium for those who know the idea of prefix sum of an array,
 and Hard for those who doesn't know what is prefix sum approach.

so my advice is, first know about prefix sum if you don't know, then try this out.

discussion_posts.name: anonymous_swe
discussion_posts.content:
I was looking over face detection and compare across a very well recognized research paper that uses this algorithm as part of the process. look under "Integral Image"

https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Michael_Jones20/publication/3940582_Rapid_Object_Detection_using_a_Boosted_Cascade_of_Simple_Features/links/0f31753b419c639337000000.pdf

discussion_posts.name: Gaurav_Mangal
discussion_posts.content: wtf [@LeetCode](/LeetCode) its showing that my last test case exceeded the time limit but it is not showing what the test case was.

discussion_posts.name: lovesbumblebees
discussion_posts.content: protip: if you make your prefix matrix bigger by one unit in both dimensions, then you can avoid coding for boundaries. You write half as much code and you don\'t need any branches in the sumRegion method.

discussion_posts.name: umeshCodesJAVA
discussion_posts.content:
Wow cool problem. Learned a new concept today!
Definitely a medium-hard one if one is seeing it for the first time, but once you know the concept you would be able to do similar problems easily.

Happy Leetcoding :)

discussion_posts.name: khanna111
discussion_posts.content: O(1) solution is hard to visualize without having seen it earlier.
